title: Test Execution and Reports
description: Create test runs and test sets, execute tests as a tester, report bugs from failed steps, and examine test results as a test engineer.

topics.title: Test Runs, Sets, and Configuration

content.text: After you have written and approved test cases, you must create test runs to execute them. You cannot execute a test case directly — you must first create a test run for the test case. This is the test setup stage of the test management workflow, typically performed by the test engineer.

Test runs are their own tracker type in Codebeamer. They are downstream references to test cases and are typically created by test engineers but executed by testers. You can configure a test run with different options including test configurations, priority, running time, build, and release.

rows: Single Test Case
A test run for one test case
Testing a specific feature or requirement in isolation
Multiple Test Cases
A test run containing several test cases
Testing related features together or quickly creating runs for each case
Test Set
A collection of test cases to execute together
Regression testing, functional area testing, or ordered test sequences
Combinations
A mix of test cases and test sets
Comprehensive testing that combines different grouping strategies

content.text: When generating a test run for multiple test cases, you can choose to create one test run for all selected cases (an ad hoc test set) or create separate test runs for each test case. The default is a single combined test run.

cards.back: A named collection of test cases to execute together. You can specify execution order (sequential) and group tests by functional area. Test sets are their own tracker type.

Defines the environment or conditions for test execution — such as vehicle models, operating systems, or hardware settings. Applied to test runs, not test cases. Multiple configurations create multiple test runs.
When a test set's Sequential field is set to True, test cases must be executed in the specified order. Use this when the order of testing matters, such as unit tests before integration tests.

content.prompt: Match each test setup concept to its description:

pairs.right: The actual execution instance of one or more test cases
A reusable collection of test cases grouped for a specific purpose
Environment parameters applied to a test run
Variable values in test steps that generate multiple test case instances

keyTakeaways: Test runs are required to execute test cases — you cannot execute a test case directly
Test runs can be created for single cases, multiple cases, test sets, or combinations
Test configurations define the execution environment and are applied to test runs, not test cases
Test sets group related test cases and can enforce sequential execution order

topics.title: Test Execution and Bug Reporting

content.text: After test runs have been created, the tester or test engineer executes them. During execution, the tester records results for each step, reports bugs, and writes conclusive remarks. After completion, test runs typically cannot be edited to maintain accuracy and traceability.

content.prompt: Arrange the test execution steps in the correct order:
items: Review the test run details and configuration
Record results for each step (pass, fail, or block)
Write a conclusive statement about the test run

content.text: During testing, bugs and issues may be reported. A bug discovered in a test run may relate to several root causes that the test engineer or test lead must evaluate.

rows: Test run setup
Unachievable test configurations or inaccurate pre-actions
Revise the test run setup or configuration
Test case definition
Inaccurate test step actions or parameters
Update the test case steps or parameters
Product problem
Failed product definition that does not meet the requirement
Initiate a change request for the product
Tester error
Incorrect execution by the tester
Re-execute the test with proper procedures

content.text: Bugs are their own tracker type in Codebeamer. You can create traceability relationships between bugs, tests, and product definitions, as well as with change requests used to address issues. This maintains a complete audit trail from requirement to defect resolution.

content.prompt: Match each step result to its meaning:

pairs.right: The actual result matches the expected result
The actual result does not match the expected result
The step cannot be executed due to external dependencies
Create a linked defect from a failed step

keyTakeaways: The tester follows a structured process: review, pre-actions, execute steps, conclusion, post-actions
Each test step is recorded as pass, fail, or block during execution
Bugs can originate from test setup, test case definitions, product problems, or tester errors
Bugs created from test runs are automatically linked to the test case and requirement for traceability

topics.title: Exercise - Create Test Runs

content.text: As a test engineer, you will create test runs for test cases using different methods: generating from a single test case, from a test case with parameters, from multiple test cases, and from a test set.
Sign in as the Test Engineer (emma / ptc). Make sure all test cases from Module 3 exercises have been created and approved.

content.title: Create Test Runs
content.objective: Generate test runs for individual test cases, parameterized test cases, multiple test cases, and test sets.

tasks.title: Generate a Test Run from a Single Test Case

steps.action: Sign out as the test lead and sign in as the test engineer with username emma and password ptc.
steps.detail: Test engineers create test runs; testers execute them.

steps.action: Navigate to the System Test Cases tracker and select Manual Headlight Switch Integration.
steps.detail: You will generate a test run for this predefined system test case.

steps.action: Click + (create new Downstream Reference) and select Generate Test Run > PTCU Electric Car TEST >> Test Runs.
steps.detail: A test run is a downstream reference to the test case it executes.

steps.action: Set the Assigned to field to the Tester role.
steps.detail: The tester will execute the test run you are creating.

steps.action: In the Test Configurations section, select both TL Model and TLX Model checkboxes.
steps.detail: These two configurations represent different vehicle models. Selecting both creates two separate test runs — one for each configuration.
steps.hint: Test configurations are applied to test runs, not test cases.

steps.action: Click Save and navigate to the new test runs.
steps.detail: Two test runs have been created because of the two test configurations. Observe the Status and Test Configuration fields.
steps.hint: Navigate via the notification or the Trackers tab.

tasks.title: Generate a Test Run for a Parameterized Test Case

steps.action: Navigate to Software Test Cases and select Test Light Sensor Communication with ECU.
steps.detail: This test case has parameters defined in its test steps.
steps.hint: Expand the Test Steps section to observe the input and output parameters.

steps.action: Click + (create new Downstream Reference) and select Generate Test Run.
steps.detail: A test run for a parameterized test case will create separate executions for each parameter set.

steps.action: Assign the test run to the Tester role and click Save.
steps.detail: The test run will have three test cases to execute — one for each input parameter row.

steps.action: Navigate to the new test run and expand the Test Results section.
steps.detail: The Remaining Test Cases field should show three test cases, reflecting the three parameter rows.

tasks.title: Generate a Test Run for Multiple Test Cases

steps.action: Navigate to Software Test Cases and multi-select Test Light Sensor Communication with ECU and Light Sensor Unit Test.
steps.detail: You can generate a combined test run from multiple selected test cases.

steps.action: Right-click and select Generate Test Run(s) from Selected > PTCU Electric Car TEST >> Test Runs.
steps.detail: The default creates one test run containing all selected test cases.
steps.hint: The 'Create one Test Run for each Test Cases' option defaults to No.

steps.action: Click Save and navigate to the new test run.
steps.detail: The test run contains four test cases: three from the parameterized test case plus one for Light Sensor Unit Test.

tasks.title: Create a Test Set and Generate a Test Run

steps.action: In Software Test Cases, select all test cases except Light Sensor Component Test. Right-click and select Create Test Set from selected Test Cases.
steps.detail: Test sets group test cases for functional area testing or regression testing.

steps.action: Set the Name to ECU and Light Sensor Unit to System and the Sequential field to True.
steps.detail: Sequential execution means test cases must run in the specified order.

steps.action: Reorder the test cases by dragging: 1) Light Sensor Unit Test, 2) Verify Unit Code for ECU Headlight Control, 3) Test Light Sensor Communication with ECU.
steps.detail: The order progresses from unit tests to system-to-system testing.

steps.action: Click Save then Close.
steps.detail: The test set is created with the specified test case order.

steps.action: Navigate to Test Sets, open the new test set, and click New Test Run (play+ icon).
steps.detail: You are creating a test run for the entire test set.

steps.action: Assign the test run to the Tester role and click Save.
steps.detail: The test run should show 5 remaining test cases (unit tests + parameterized system test).

keyTakeaways: Test runs can be generated from single test cases, multiple cases, or test sets
Applying multiple test configurations creates separate test runs for each configuration
Parameterized test cases generate multiple execution instances within a single test run
Test sets can enforce sequential execution order from unit tests to system tests

topics.title: Exercise - Execute Test Runs

content.text: As the tester, you will execute different test runs — including runs with and without parameters — record pass/fail results, report a bug from a failed step, and optionally execute a test set run.
Sign in as the Tester (yuta / ptc). Make sure all test runs from the previous exercise have been created.

content.title: Execute Test Runs
content.objective: Execute test runs with and without parameters, record results, report bugs, and optionally execute a test set.

tasks.title: Execute a Test Run Without Parameters

steps.action: Sign in with username yuta and password ptc.
steps.detail: Yuta has the Tester role for executing test runs.

steps.action: Navigate to the Test Runs tracker. Add a filter for Assigned to: Tester and click GO.
steps.detail: Filtering shows only the test runs assigned to your role.

steps.action: Open one of the Quick Test Run for Manual Headlight Switch Integration test runs and note the Test Configuration.
steps.detail: There are two test runs with the same name — one for each test configuration (TL Model and TLX Model).
steps.hint: Prior to execution, the tester would set up the test configuration and perform any pre-actions.

steps.action: Click Run from the toolbar. Read the Description and Pre-Action fields.
steps.detail: Understanding the pre-actions is essential before beginning test execution.

steps.action: For step 1, click Pass Step. For step 2, click Pass Step.
steps.detail: The Actual Result field is automatically filled with the Expected result content when you pass a step.

steps.action: For step 3, click Fail Step.
steps.detail: This simulates a test failure that needs to be reported as a bug.

steps.action: Click Report Bug. Verify the Project is set to PTCU Electric Car TEST and Tracker is set to Bugs. Click Next.
steps.detail: Creating a bug directly from a failed step automatically links the bug to the test case and requirement.

steps.action: Observe the Test Steps section results and click Save.
steps.detail: The bug is created with the failed step details.

steps.action: For Conclusion, type Headlights did not turn off in off position and click Save.
steps.detail: The conclusion summarizes the test run outcome.

steps.action: Observe the Result and Reported bug(s) fields, and the Test Results section.
steps.detail: The test run is now complete with recorded results and a linked bug.

tasks.title: Execute a Test Run with Parameters

steps.action: Navigate to the Test Runs tracker and select Quick Test Run for Test Light Sensor Communication with ECU.
steps.detail: This test run has parameterized test cases. The executed test run from above is no longer visible because of the filter.

steps.action: Observe the Test Results section showing three remaining test cases, then click Run.
steps.detail: Three test cases correspond to the three parameter rows defined in the test case.

steps.action: Click Select in the parameter box to view the parameters, then click Cancel.
steps.detail: Each parameter set defines different input and output values for the test step.

steps.action: Click Pass Step for the first parameter. Leave the Conclusion blank and click Save.
steps.detail: The parameter box updates to the second parameter automatically.

steps.action: Observe the second parameter's values in the Action and Expected result fields. Click Pass Step and Save.
steps.detail: Each parameter generates a separate test case execution with its own input/output values.

steps.action: Repeat for the third parameter: click Pass Step and Save.
steps.detail: All three parameter-based test cases are now executed. Check the Test Results section for the Executed Test Cases value.

steps.action: Expand the test case in the Tests below were/will be executed section and the Details of Test Results section.
steps.detail: The three parameter-based test cases appear as children of the parent test case.

steps.action: Navigate to the Test Runs tracker. Remove all filters except Assigned to and click GO.
steps.detail: Previously executed test runs are no longer in the default view. Adjusting filters shows all assigned test runs.

steps.action: Select Test Run for ECU and Light Sensor Unit to System and observe the five test cases in the Test Results section.
steps.detail: The test set contains unit tests and a parameterized system test, totaling five test case executions.

steps.action: Click Run. Pass all steps for the first test case and Save.
steps.detail: Notice the counter (e.g., 1 of 5) in the upper-right corner tracking progress.

steps.action: For the second test case, click the dropdown in the Pass Steps block and select Pass Remaining Steps. Save.
steps.detail: Pass Remaining Steps is a shortcut to quickly pass all steps in a test case.

steps.action: Click Pause Run and type need to setup in the Comment field. Click Yes.
steps.detail: You can pause a test execution and return to it later. Test execution data cannot be modified once a test case is completed.

steps.action: Observe the test run Status is set to In Progress.
steps.detail: The paused test run retains its progress and can be resumed later.

keyTakeaways: Test execution records pass, fail, or block for each step and generates a conclusion
Bugs created from failed steps are automatically linked to the test case and requirement
Parameterized test cases execute once for each parameter row with different input/output values
Test runs can be paused and resumed — completed test cases cannot be modified after execution

topics.title: Exercise - Examine Test Results

content.text: As the test engineer, you will monitor test run progress using the Test Run Browser, review statistics, and examine bugs reported during test execution.

content.title: Examine Test Results
content.objective: Use the Test Run Browser to review test results and examine reported bugs.

tasks.title: View Test Results in the Test Run Browser

steps.action: Sign in as the test engineer with username emma and password ptc.
steps.detail: The test engineer or test lead examines test run progress and evaluates reported defects.

steps.action: Navigate to the Test Runs tracker and select Test Run Browser from the toolbar.
steps.detail: The Test Run Browser provides a consolidated view of all test run results.

steps.action: Set the Number of recent Test Runs shown field to 10 and click GO.
steps.detail: This ensures all recent test runs are visible in the browser.

steps.action: Locate the TEST RUN BROWSER STATISTICS section and observe the results.
steps.detail: The statistics show pass/fail rates across all test runs.

steps.action: Expand PTCU Electric Car TEST >> System Test Cases >> Manual Headlight Switch Integration and observe the Result column.
steps.detail: You can see which test runs passed and which failed for each test case.

steps.action: Expand PTCU Electric Car TEST >> Test Sets >> ECU and Light Sensor Unit to System and observe the results.
steps.detail: Test set results show the aggregate outcome of all test cases in the set.

tasks.title: View Reported Bugs from Test Runs

steps.action: Navigate to the Bugs tracker via the Trackers tab.
steps.detail: Bugs reported during test execution appear in the Bugs tracker.

steps.action: Select Bug for "Manual Headlight Switch Integration" and observe the failed step details.
steps.detail: The bug includes the failed step information and traceability links back to the test case and requirement.

steps.action: Review the bug's associations to confirm traceability to the test case and requirement.
steps.detail: The complete traceability chain connects: Requirement → Test Case → Test Run → Bug. This chain makes it clear which requirement is impacted by this defect.

keyTakeaways: The Test Run Browser provides a consolidated view of test results with pass/fail statistics
Test engineers and test leads use results to evaluate defects and determine next steps
Bugs maintain traceability links back through the test case to the original requirement
The complete traceability chain (Requirement → Test Case → Test Run → Bug) enables impact analysis
